subject: Geography
title: Lagos: Africa's Megacity
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Lagos: Africa's Megacity.

hook.text: Lagos grows by over 3,000 people every day. It may reach 100 million by century's end.

buildup.text: Nigeria's economic capital sprawls across lagoons, swamps, and reclaimed islands.

discovery.text: Makoko, a floating slum on Lagos Lagoon, houses 300,000 people in stilt houses over water.

twist.text: Despite chaos, Lagos generates more GDP than most African nations combined.

climax.text: Lagos is a preview of Africa's urban future—explosive growth outpacing infrastructure.

punchline.text: The fastest-growing city on Earth can't build fast enough.

quiz.question: How many people move to Lagos each day?
